#domain/reliability #domain/data-integrity

## Silent truncation at a length budget drops the tail without error

When a value crosses a character/byte ceiling and the system trims-to-fit
instead of rejecting, the tail vanishes with no exception — the caller believes
the full value went through.

- The hazard is the *silence*: no error, no warning, just a shorter value.
- It surfaces far from the cut — wrong search results, missing text, mismatched
  comparisons — making it hard to trace back to the truncation point.
- Same failure mode recurs across stacks: SQL `SET ANSI_WARNINGS OFF` trims on
  insert; Dapper's `DbString.Length` trims the bound value; a string slice in a
  generator drops the overflow.
- Fix shape: validate length at the boundary and **fail loudly** (reject or warn)
  rather than quietly fit.

**From this session:** `pick-up`'s 141-char description hit
`firstSentence(desc, 140)`, which sliced to 139 chars and appended `…`, dropping
the tail with no error — had to trim the source to ≤140.

Related: [[Fail loud at system boundaries]], [[Validate input length before processing]]
Sources: https://www.sqlshack.com/sql-truncate-enhancement-silent-data-truncation-in-sql-server-2019/, https://github.com/DapperLib/Dapper/issues/1390
